popularStory.headline:
Jane Cooke Mitchell
(1605 – 18 June 1666)
popularStory.description:
Jane was born about 1604 and probably in Leiden, Holland.

Jane married Experience Mitchell, by about 1628 (no marriage record has been found). 

She widowed him. They had children:

Elizabeth 1628

T …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Cooke於大約1604年出生在荷蘭霍蘭萊登時，她的父親Francis Cooke23歲，母親Hester Mahieu21歲。她於1627年5月22日之後與Experience Mitchell在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她於1623年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。她去世於1640年之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Cooke于大约1604年出生在荷兰霍蘭萊登时，她的父亲Francis Cooke23岁，母亲Hester Mahieu21岁。她于1627年5月22日之后与Experience Mitchell在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她于1623年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。她去世于1640年之前在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。

nameMeanings.name: Cooke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Cook  .

History: Francis Cooke (died 1663) and his eldest son John were passengers on the Mayflower in 1621; they were joined two years later by Francis's wife and other children. In the words of William Bradford, he lived long enough ‘to see his children's children have children’.

variants.name: Cook
meanings: English: occupational name for a cook, a seller of cooked meats, or a keeper of an eating house, from Middle English cok, coke, cook, couk, cuk(e) (Old English cōc) ‘cook’ or ‘seller of cooked foods’. See also Kew .
Irish and Scottish: usually identical in origin with the English name (see 1 above), but in some cases a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cúg ‘son of Hugo’ (see McCook ).

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘cook’, such as German and Jewish Koch , Dutch Kook , Polish Kucharz and Kucharczyk , Slovenian and Croatian Kuhar , North German Kuk .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.description: Rembrandt Harmenszoon van Rijn was born in Leiden, the Netherlands.
"English navigator Henry Hudson, under contract to the United East India Company, sails from Amsterdam and ""discovers""Manhattan Island and the future site of New York."
In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Cooke was born about 1604, in Leiden, Holland, Netherlands, her father, Francis Cooke, was 23 and her mother, Hester Mahieu, was 21. She married Experience Mitchell after 22 May 1627, in Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She immigrated to Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America in 1623. She died before 1640, in Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Cooke est née environ 1604, à Leiden, Holland, Pays-Bas, son père, Francis Cooke, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Hester Mahieu, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé Experience Mitchell après 22 mai 1627, à Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle a immigré au Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1623. Elle est décédée avant 1640, à Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Cooke è nata il circa 1604 nel Leiden, Holland, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Francis Cooke, aveva 23 anni e sua madre, Hester Mahieu, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato Experience Mitchell dopo il 22 maggio 1627, nel Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Immigrata nel Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America nel 1623. Morì prima del 1640, nel Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ko.Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colon: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 플림우스

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Cooke(이)가 대략 1604년 네덜란드 홀랜드 레이든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Francis Cooke(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Hester Mahieu(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1627년 5월 22일 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이에서 Experience Mitchell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1623년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이로 이민을 떠났다. 그녀는 1640년 이전 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Cooke nasceu aproximadamente 1604, em Leiden, Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Francis Cooke, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Hester Mahieu, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com Experience Mitchell após 22 de maio de 1627, em Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela imigrou para o Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America em 1623. Ela faleceu antes 1640, em Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Cooke родилась около 1604 г. в местности лиден, холланд, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Francis Cooke было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Hester Mahieu, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Experience Mitchell после 22 май 1627 г. в местности плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она иммигрировала в плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1623 году. Она умерла перед 1640 г. в местности плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.
